questions.question: Explain the concept of recursion with an example. Differentiate between base case and recursive case.
keyPoints: Recursion is a technique where function calls itself
Base case is the terminating condition that stops recursion
Recursive case is where function calls itself with modified parameters
Example: factorial function demonstrates both base case (n=0 or 1 returns 1) and recursive case (n * factorial(n-1))

questions.question: Write a recursive C function to calculate the sum of array elements. Explain the logic.
keyPoints: Function takes array and size as parameters
Base case: when size is 0, return 0
Recursive case: return first element plus sum of remaining elements
Time complexity: O(n), Space complexity: O(n)

questions.question: What is the difference between direct and indirect recursion? Give one example of each.
keyPoints: Direct recursion: function calls itself directly
Indirect recursion: function A calls function B, which calls A
Example of direct: factorial function
Example of indirect: function A calls B, B calls A

questions.question: What is tail recursion? How does it differ from non-tail recursion?
keyPoints: Tail recursion: recursive call is the last statement in the function
Compiler can optimize tail recursion by reusing stack frame
Non-tail recursion: operations pending after recursive call returns
Tail recursion converts to iteration efficiently

questions.question: Explain the Tower of Hanoi problem. Write the recursive algorithm for moving n disks from source to destination using auxiliary peg.
keyPoints: Problem: move n disks from source to destination using auxiliary peg
Rules: only one disk moved at a time, larger disk cannot be on smaller disk
Recursive solution: move n-1 disks to auxiliary, move largest to destination, move n-1 to destination
Minimum moves required: 2^n - 1

questions.question: What is stack overflow in recursion? What are its causes and how can it be prevented?
keyPoints: Stack overflow occurs when call stack exhausts its memory limit
Cause: too many recursive calls without proper termination
Prevention: always have a valid base case
Alternative: convert to iteration or increase stack size

questions.question: Write a recursive C function to check whether a string is a palindrome.
keyPoints: Function takes string and two indices (start and end)
Base case: if start >= end, return 1 (true)
Base case: if characters don't match, return 0 (false)
Recursive case: check next pair with start+1 and end-1

questions.question: Write a C program to implement binary search using recursion. Explain the algorithm and analyze its time and space complexity.
keyPoints: Algorithm: divide sorted array in half, search in appropriate half
Function parameters: array, low, high, key
Base cases: element found OR search space exhausted
Time complexity: O(log n), Space complexity: O(log n) due to recursion stack
Code must handle edge cases like empty array and element not found

questions.question: Explain the naive recursive Fibonacci algorithm and discuss its problems. How can memoization improve its efficiency?
keyPoints: Naive algorithm: F(n) = F(n-1) + F(n-2) with base cases F(0)=0, F(1)=1
Problem: exponential time O(2^n) due to overlapping subproblems
Example: F(3) is calculated multiple times for larger inputs
Memoization: store computed values in array, reuse when needed
With memoization: time complexity reduces to O(n), space O(n) for array

questions.question: Compare recursion and iteration. When should one be preferred over the other? Provide suitable examples.
keyPoints: Iteration uses loops, recursion uses function calls
Recursion: better for inherently recursive problems (trees, graphs, divide-and-conquer)
Iteration: more memory efficient, no stack overflow risk
Recursion provides cleaner, more readable code for complex problems
Examples: traversal (recursion), linear search (iteration)
Tail recursion optimization can make recursion as efficient as iteration

questions.question: Write a C program to reverse a string using recursion. Also implement it to reverse a singly linked list recursively.
keyPoints: String reversal: swap characters from两端, move toward center
Function parameters: string, start, end indices
Base case: when start >= end, string is reversed
Linked list reversal: recursively traverse to end, then reverse pointers
For linked list: base case is NULL or single node
Time complexity: O(n), Space complexity: O(n) for both
